description_long: This is a vintage biplane with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by vertical struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, with an open cockpit situated near the front, just behind a wooden two-blade propeller. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The exterior is finished in a light tan color with some darker accents, and the structure appears to be primarily wood and fabric, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The overall design is lightweight and utilitarian, emphasizing function over modern aerodynamic refinement.
description: A vintage biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear, finished in tan and dark accents. The design is lightweight, with a conventional tail and visible struts and wires.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and wooden propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the wing struts, open cockpit, and the fixed main landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the rear of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the tail skid, wing structure, and the open cockpit from behind.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the propeller; appears to be single-seat with no visible canopy.
